meta.title: Laravel Core Rules
meta.description: Senior production standards for Laravel applications. Compatible with MergeCore deterministic and LLM-assisted review.
meta.notes: Confirm each finding against framework version, installed packages, and project conventions. Penalties are applied by the host engine; this file defines intent and traceability.

error.label: High risk or incorrect framework use

scoring.normalisation: After summing penalties for matched rules in one review unit, score = max(0, min(10, initial_score - min(sum_penalties, max_total_penalty_per_file))).

interpretation.9_to_10: Production-ready for the reviewed surface: clear boundaries, safe queries, and operable failure modes.
interpretation.7_to_8: Mergeable with targeted follow-ups: tighten validation, policies, or query shape.
interpretation.5_to_6: Risky merge: likely bugs, authorisation gaps, or performance traps.
interpretation.0_to_4: Do not merge without rework: security, correctness, or operational risk.

rules.title: Bloated controller action
rules.description: Controllers should orchestrate HTTP concerns. Heavy domain logic belongs in actions, services, or domain classes to keep requests testable and cohesive.

heuristics: Multiple database writes in one action

examples.before: public function store(Request $request) { /* validation, persistence, mail, analytics in one block */ }

rules.title: Mass assignment from all input
rules.description: Using $request->all() or unvalidated arrays for mass assignment is a common source of privilege escalation and unintended column updates.

rules.title: Inline validation only in controller
rules.description: Repeated $request->validate([...]) scatters rules and blocks reuse from jobs, commands, and CLI entry points.

rules.title: Authorisation missing on mutation
rules.description: Destructive routes should be gated by policies, gates, or middleware that map to explicit abilities.

rules.title: Authorisation logic duplicated in controllers
rules.description: Repeated role or ownership checks drift from policies and make refactors hazardous.

rules.title: N+1 query risk in loops
rules.description: Accessing lazy-loaded relations inside a loop causes N+1 queries and unstable latency under load.

rules.title: Unbounded query for user-facing lists
rules.description: Missing pagination or limits risks memory exhaustion and slow responses.

context: Controller or query returning large tables

rules.title: Raw SQL without safe bindings
rules.description: DB::raw / whereRaw with string concatenation invites SQL injection.

rules.title: Non-idempotent job side effects
rules.description: Retries can duplicate charges, e-mails, or rows unless the handler is idempotent or guarded by unique constraints.

heuristics: External side effects without idempotency key

rules.title: Heavy work on the request thread
rules.description: Long outbound calls in HTTP requests hurt tail latency; queue with explicit failure handling where appropriate.

rules.title: Queue job without failure strategy
rules.description: Jobs should define sensible retries/backoff and failed handling; silent loss is a production incident waiting to happen.

heuristics: Missing failed() or logging on terminal failure

rules.title: Multi-step monetary movement without transaction
rules.description: Ledger-like updates spanning multiple writes should be atomic.

rules.title: Long transaction holding locks
rules.description: Outbound HTTP or slow I/O inside DB::transaction increases deadlock risk.

rules.title: Implicit lazy loading in API resources
rules.description: Resources touching relations without eager loading cause N+1 in production.

rules.title: Tenant scope bypass
rules.description: Queries without a tenant constraint can leak data across organisations when the schema is multi-tenant.

heuristics: Global query without tenant guard where tenant_id exists

rules.title: Tenant identifier from untrusted input
rules.description: Client-supplied tenant identifiers must not replace membership resolution from authentication context.

rules.title: Webhook handler not idempotent
rules.description: Providers retry webhooks; dedupe by event id or stored delivery state before applying side effects.

rules.title: Explicit uniqueness for queue work
rules.description: ShouldBeUnique or WithoutOverlapping reduces duplicate side effects under concurrency.

rules.title: Filament query without intended scoping
rules.description: Admin tables must respect tenant and authorisation scopes; accidental global queries leak rows.

rules.title: Heavy side effects in Filament form callbacks
rules.description: Large work in afterStateUpdated is hard to test and blocks the UI; prefer actions or queued jobs.

rules.title: Feature test missing auth expectations
rules.description: Protected routes need guest/forbidden assertions; happy-path-only tests miss authorisation regressions.

rules.title: Database tests without isolation strategy
rules.description: Use RefreshDatabase or an equivalent strategy to avoid order-dependent failures.

rules.title: Mass assignment of guarded attributes
rules.description: Ensure $fillable/$guarded and DTOs align; never rely on obscurity for sensitive columns.

heuristics: User-controlled keys merged into models without validated subset

rules.title: Secrets or tokens in logs
rules.description: Logging request payloads or tokens breaches compliance and aids attackers.

rules.title: Public mutation route without throttling
rules.description: Sensitive POST/PUT/PATCH endpoints should use rate limiting middleware where abuse is plausible.

heuristics: Route::post without throttle middleware in high-risk contexts

rules.title: Explicit eager loads at the boundary
rules.description: with() for rendered relations is a strong signal of deliberate N+1 mitigation.

rules.title: Excessive indirection for trivial flows
rules.description: Many layers for simple CRUD slows change without adding invariants; match abstraction to complexity.

heuristics: Many classes for a single-field update
